steps.title: Identify the text's key signal about Pallikkathayil's role
steps.content: The passage sets up a puzzle: if the intuition that lying can be permissible is correct, what justifies opposition to lying at all? It then says Pallikkathayil “has advanced one answer“ to this puzzle. The word “one” signals that his framework is a potential justification being offered as a possible solution.

steps.title: Match the framework to the choices
steps.content: Pallikkathayil's argument provides a framework for justifying opposition to lies through respect for others' agency, while permitting exceptions when truthfulness causes greater harm—this is exactly what C expresses with “one potential means” and “except in certain circumstances.” A contradicts the passage by claiming an inviolable duty, B reverses the passage by suggesting grounds are unclear, and D mistakes its philosophical aim for a practical one. That matches choice C — C is correct.
